page: Microsoft Azure D48as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at 3.7 GHz. The configuration includes 48 virtual CPUs, which correspond to 24 physical cores with 2 threads per core, and 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates strong compute capabilities. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng ranks in the top 10%, while multi-core performance and Geekbench scores fall within the top 25%. Memory performance is mixed; small-block cached read-only bandwidth is strong, whereas large-block uncached read and write bandwidths remain in the average tier. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as Passmark encryption and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, show strong performance. For LLM inference, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds with small 135M models, but performance drops to the bottom 25% when executing text generation on large 70B models.

The absence of local storage requires reliance on external Premium Storage, which represents a key architectural tradeoff for disk-heavy operations. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D48as_v6 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads that require high CPU performance without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. Appropriate workloads include software compilation, Redis caching, web serving, and moderate database operations.
description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with a dedicated 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor featuring 48 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM, maintaining a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core execution, alongside strong small-block cached memory bandwidth. However, large-block uncached memory bandwidth and large-model LLM text generation are average to weak. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for general-purpose workloads, offering balanced resource density. It is suited for software compilation, Redis operations, and web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring 48 vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, 192 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong single-core and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9V74 CPU, offering strong multi-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and high-density memory for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core benchmark results
No local storage included, requiring external Premium Storage solutions
Suited for software compilation, Redis caching, and general-purpose web serving
